title: rhinanthus serotinus subsp. apterus
intro: Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. apterus, also known as the late-flowering or early-flowering serotine or red rattle in some regions, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. apterus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve coughs and colds, and alleviate digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to address various health concerns.
